data.title: PPA vs. ITA vs. XAR – Which Defense ETF Is Best?
data.description:
As geopolitical tensions and defense budgets continue to impact markets, exchange-traded funds focused on the aerospace and defense sector have drawn investor attention. Three of the largest and most widely followed ETFs in this space are the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), the iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), and the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR). While all three provide access to defense contractors and related aerospace businesses, they differ in structure, diversification, and strategy.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF

The Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF tracks the SPADEX Defense Index and focuses on companies whose primary business is defense or aerospace work. As a result, it tends to have heavier exposure to major defense contractors such as Lockheed Martin (LMT), Raytheon (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC). Because of this focused approach, PPA is often closely impacted by changes in defense spending and geopolitical developments. However, this concentration can also lead to more volatility when government budgets shift.

iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF

The iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF tracks the Dow Jones U.S. Select Aerospace & Defense Index and provides exposure to a broader mix of companies. In addition to prime defense contractors, it includes suppliers, parts manufacturers, and aerospace service providers. Because of this wider industrial footprint, ITA can benefit not only from defense spending but also from broader aerospace demand. This structure may help smooth performance when defense budgets are not the only growth driver.

SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF

The SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF uses an equal-weight methodology rather than market-cap weighting. This means that smaller and mid-sized companies receive similar initial weightings as the largest defense contractors. As a result, XAR provides more balanced exposure across the industry and is less dominated by a handful of major names. This approach can potentially capture growth from emerging suppliers and mid-tier firms.

Wall Street's Take on Defense ETFs

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $326.66 per share implies 20.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Tesla Stock: Energy Boom vs. EV Bust – Can FSD Drive 2026 Recovery?
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) traded around $411-$420 in early 2026, down 7% year-to-date amid EV sales declines. Yet, Tesla's most bullish analyst eyes potential 40%+ upside from energy storage growth and Full Self-Driving (FSD) monetization.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Challenges Weigh Heavy

Tesla's Q4 FY25 deliveries dropped 15% year-over-year to 418,000 units, marking a second annual decline. Fierce competition from Chinese rival BYD (BYDDF) and softening demand in Europe, where registrations fell over 40%, have led to the declines. Leadership shake-ups, including a new global sales head, highlight internal efforts to counter eroding market share and margin-squeezing price wars.

Energy Business Powers Ahead

Energy storage deployments hit records in 2025, positioning Megapack and Powerwall for $12 to $15 billion in revenue by 2027, diversifying beyond vehicles hit by macroeconomic headwinds. This high-margin segment could offset auto weakness, with 2025 revenue at $95 billion despite a 2.9% dip. Investors see it as a stabilizing force amid Tesla's pivot to software-led growth.

FSD and Robotics to the Rescue

Tesla's FSD subscriptions could generate $50-$100 monthly per vehicle, potentially lifting margins to 13%-20% by 2028 if regulatory approvals accelerate. Tesla's $20 billion 2026 capex guidance targets autonomous driving and Optimus robots. CEO Elon Musk plans to end production of premium Model S and Model X at its California factories to reallocate capacity to Optimus.

Analysts' Views on Tesla Stock

Tigress Financial analyst Ivan Feinseth initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $550 price target, implying 31.9% upside potential. He sees Tesla shifting its focus from a pure-play EV maker to a broad AI platform featuring self-driving tech. He noted that growth will come from more FSD subscriptions, robotaxis, and Optimus robots, adding to its strong EV and energy businesses.

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintained his Hold rating and $415 price target, suggesting that shares are fully valued at current levels. He sees Tesla's solar manufacturing push as a smart long-term bet on geopolitics and data center demand. At full capacity, Tesla Solar could add $20 billion to $50 billion in value ($6 to $14 per share) to its $140 billion energy business ($40 per share). Although not huge on a standalone basis, vertical integration could boost growth.

Is Tesla Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Hershey Stock (HSY) Is a Winner Heading into Valentine's Day, Up 22% on the Year
data.description:
The stock of chocolate maker Hershey Co. (HSY) is on a winning streak heading into Valentine's Day on Feb. 14, having risen 22% this year.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HSY stock has been on a blistering rally in the lead-up to Valentine's Day, one of the biggest catalysts of the year for the company behind Hershey Kisses and Reese's Peanut Butter Cups. A CouponFollow survey of 1,005 Americans in relationships found that they plan to spend an average of $87 on Valentine's Day this year, with about a quarter (25%) of that money going toward chocolate.

For Hershey, the first six weeks of 2026 have marked a big turnaround for the company and its share price. HSY stock had struggled for years as cocoa prices spiked and its input costs rose sharply. This forced Hershey to raise prices, which dented its sales and sent its share price lower.

Cocoa Prices Retreat

While Valentine's Day sales give Hershey a boost each year, the real reason its stock is on an upswing is that cocoa prices have fallen to their lowest level in two years. Cocoa's price hit a record high of $12,987 a tonne in late 2024 due to crop failures in West Africa.

But now, the cocoa crop in Africa has stabilized and cocoa's price has fallen sharply on signs of strong global supplies. This has provided much relief to Hershey and other chocolate makers. Management at Hershey recently forecast 4% to 5% sales growth this year, and full-year earnings of $8.20 to $8.52, a 35% increase from 2025.

Is HSY Stock a Buy?

Hershey stock has a consensus Hold rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on four Buy and 14 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HSY price target of $229.81 implies 3.25% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Is Gold Up Today, 2/13/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is up by over 2% on Friday and has reclaimed the $5,000 level after a cooler-than-expected inflation print led to rising rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit when rates are lower, as the opportunity cost of holding the precious metal relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, decreases.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On an annual basis, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) fell to 2.4% in January from 2.7% in December, marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. January's core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was 2.5%, the lowest level since March 2021.

Gold Rebounds as Rate Cut Hopes Grow

While a rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 is unlikely, the odds of a 25 bps reduction at the April 29 meeting climbed to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. The odds are even higher at the June 17 meeting, sitting at 52.3% from 48.9% yesterday.

Gold's move today "suggests the correction may have overshot, with bargain‑hunting and position‑adjustments now providing support," said ING Bank commodity strategist Ewa Manthey. The precious metal has returned 16.4% year-to-date and has completely recovered its losses over the past week.

data.title: Amazon-Backed (AMZN) X-Energy Secures First U.S. Nuclear Fuel License in 50 Years
data.description:
X-Energy Reactor, a privately held nuclear company backed by Amazon (AMZN), has received the first nuclear fuel license issued in the United States in more than 50 years.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The license allows X-Energy to manufacture uranium fuel for use in nuclear reactors. The nuclear fuel license is good for the next 40 years and will allow X-Energy to produce nuclear fuel using enriched uranium at two facilities in Tennessee.

X-Energy said that it plans to begin making the nuclear fuel in 2028. "Regulatory approval brings us one step closer to a resilient, American fuel supply for next-generation nuclear technology, advancing our energy security by closing a longstanding gap in the U.S. nuclear fuel cycle," said the company in a statement.

A Win for Amazon

The nuclear fuel license obtained by X-Energy is seen as a win for Amazon and other technology companies that are turning to nuclear power as a clean energy source to power their artificial intelligence (AI) data centers.

Microsoft (MSFT) has floated the idea of restarting the Three Mile Island nuclear site to power its AI data centers in future years. Amazon has a strategic partnership with X-Energy, including a $500 million investment, to develop and deploy small modular reactors (SMRs) to power its energy-intensive AI data centers with carbon-free nuclear energy.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 41 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $282.14 implies 41.93% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Rosenblatt Upgrades Roku Stock, JPMorgan Reiterates Top Pick Status on Robust Outlook
data.description:
Roku (ROKU) stock rallied more than 8% on Friday after the video streaming platform reported solid Q4 results and issued strong guidance. Impressed by the performance, several analysts raised their price targets for ROKU stock. In fact, Rosenblatt analyst Barton Crockett upgraded ROKU stock to Buy from Hold and raised his price target to $118 from $106. Also, JPMorgan analyst Cory Carpenter reaffirmed a Buy rating on ROKU stock with a price target of $125, saying that it remains a Top Pick.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rosenblatt Turns Bullish on ROKU Stock

Crockett noted that Roku "handily" surpassed the Street's Q4 expectations and provided upbeat guidance for Q1 2026 and the full year. The 4-star analyst believes that the company's outlook "looks beatable again."

Trading at an EV/EBITDA multiple of about 16x, Crockett finds ROKU stock "too cheap," given that the company's EBITDA is currently projected to grow at a 46% compound annual growth rate (2025-2027), free cash flow is currently above EBITDA, and the balance sheet has no debt and $2.3 billion in cash and investments.

"Big picture – Roku has built a formidable gatekeeper presence for U.S. streaming – half of all streaming in the U.S. on TVs goes through their devices," said Crockett. The analyst noted that Roku's ability to monetize this growth opportunity is also improving as the company is launching new monetization capabilities through Amazon (AMZN) and ramping up a new ad tool for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Crockett added that Roku is also retooling its front page for advanced ads. Moreover, he is bullish on ROKU stock due to growing streaming engagement and the international expansion potential.

JPMorgan Is Bullish on ROKU Stock

JPMorgan's Carpenter stated that his firm made Roku its Top Pick for 2026, given its optimism about acceleration in the company's Platform revenue growth. He highlighted that Roku's Platform revenue growth outlook of 18% for 2026 exceeded the Street's 15% estimate.

The analyst is optimistic about the possibility of Roku delivering Platform revenue growth of over 20%, supported by tailwinds from the Amazon DSP deal and a robust events calendar (U.S. midterms, World Cup, Winter Olympics). He also highlighted other drivers like Roku's Ads Manager offering and subscriptions (Frndly, Howdy, Premium Subs), coupled with a "steady expense base."

Is ROKU Stock a Good Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street is bullish on ROKU stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 19 Buys and one Hold recommendation. The average ROKU stock price target of $129 indicates 43.2% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Advance on Optimistic Inflation Data ahead of Extended Holiday Weekend
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Friday ahead of a three-day weekend for the stock market in honor of Presidents' Day. The market will reopen on Tuesday, February 17.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors received an encouraging inflation update after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that January's Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 0.2% from the prior month, undercutting the consensus estimate of 0.3%. CPI rose by 2.4% year-over-year, also below the expectation of 2.5% and registering the slowest pace of growth since May 2025. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 0.3% month-over-month and 2.5% year-over-year, both of which were in line with the estimates.

Falling inflation supports the case for the Fed to lower interest rates. While the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting remain low, the same odds at the next meeting on April 29 have risen to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago on the CME FedWatch tool.

Meanwhile, the Pentagon published a document showing the addition of Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), BYD (BYDDY), and several other Chinese companies to the Section 1260H list this morning. The list identifies firms that the Pentagon believes are associated with the Chinese military. However, the Pentagon took down the document about an hour later, with some speculating that the U.S. sought to ease tensions with Beijing ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in April.

"An agency letter requesting withdrawal of this document was received after placement on public inspection," wrote the Federal Register in an editorial note without providing additional details.

Elsewhere, the Trump administration is reportedly planning on easing tariffs on some aluminum and steel imports. President Trump imposed a 50% tariff on imports of the two metals last June. Since then, aluminum prices have surged 20%, while steel has climbed about 4%, adding to affordability concerns across the U.S.

However, White House trade adviser Peter Navarro denied that the U.S. would lower the tariffs, saying that Trump would maintain a "no exemptions, no exclusions" policy. Furthermore, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC that "If anything is done, I think it would be some sort of clarification on some incidental objects, but again, that's going to be the president's decision."

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.05% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.18%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Is Up 66% — How Much Higher Can the Stock Go from Here?
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) stock climbed about 66% in 2025, making it one of the strongest performers among large technology companies. Even after that rally, some analysts believe the upside may not be over. The most optimistic forecast points to about 35% more upside from current levels, based on Canaccord Genuity analyst Maria Ripps' Street-high $415 price target and a Buy rating. More broadly, Wall Street analysts collectively rate the stock a Strong Buy, with an average price target around $377, implying roughly 22% potential upside.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ripps' Take on Alphabet Stock

Ripps' view centers on one main point. She believes artificial intelligence is strengthening Alphabet's core business rather than disrupting it.

She noted that improvements from Gemini 3 are helping users better understand search results and handle more complex queries. That keeps users engaged longer and supports advertising demand, which remains the company's largest revenue source. In other words, AI tools are enhancing the search experience instead of replacing it.

At the same time, Alphabet's cloud division is gaining momentum. The analyst highlighted strong growth and a rapidly expanding backlog, reflecting rising enterprise demand for Google's AI tools and custom chips. Adoption of Gemini Enterprise has also grown quickly, with millions of paid seats already in use.

AI Spending Raises Debate

Alphabet plans a major increase in capital spending, guiding for roughly $175 billion to $185 billion in investments. The large outlay has raised some investor concern about near-term margins.

However, Ripps views the spending as a sign that management is confident about the long-term payoff from AI investments. In her view, the company is becoming not only an ad business but also a provider of AI computing and business software.

What Lies Ahead for GOOGL Stock

Looking forward, investors will likely focus on three areas: search engagement, cloud growth, and the pace of AI adoption. Continued improvements in search usage would support ad revenue, while expanding cloud deals could show that enterprises are adopting Google's AI tools at scale.

For now, the key question is whether higher spending can translate into sustained revenue growth. Analysts generally believe it can, but the next few quarters will show whether AI investment boosts sales quickly enough to offset near-term costs.

Is Alphabet Stock a Good Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 26 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average Alphabet price target of $377.23 per share implies 22.08% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Slides to 2-Month Low as Inflation Drifts Down
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 4 bps to 4.064% on Friday, marking the lowest level since December 2025.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A lower-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading for January contributed to the decline. Inflation rose by 2.4%, below the estimate of 2.5% and marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. Easing inflation strengthens the case for interest rate cuts, increasing demand for bonds and pushing yields lower.

Why Is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Down Today?

While the Fed doesn't directly control long-term yields, cooler inflation lowers expectations for future interest rates and reduces inflation risk, making Treasuries more attractive to investors.

The central bank is scheduled to meet for its next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) on March 18. The odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the meeting are slim, with the CME FedWatch tool pricing in a 9.8% chance. However, those odds increase to 26.4% at the next meeting on April 29.

data.title: Film Pros Plan "Tooth and Nail" Fight against Warner / Netflix Deal, Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips
data.description:
Entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) may be facing a proxy fight over the deal to be bought by Netflix (NFLX), but it may have an even bigger fight on its hands from the film industry itself. Stuart Ford, a major name in the independent film circuit, has some dire pronouncements and deep concerns about the state of the film industry should this deal go through. Warner shareholders seemed concerned as well, and shares slipped fractionally in the closing minutes of Friday's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stuart Ford is chairman of AGC Studios, and he made it clear that a Netflix/Warner deal would be a disaster. Sufficient disaster, in fact, to require the strongest possible response. Basically, a deal like that would turn entertainment professionals into the equivalent of an "Uber (UBER) driver" that would cost the entire industry talent. Ford called on professionals to "…fight tooth and nail to preserve the business culture of money flowing through the system…."

Ford further noted, "We're only just now starting to feel the effects in the industry of the flow of money having become interrupted, if not completely cut off, over the last 10 years as a result of streaming becoming such a major part of the distribution pipeline." The biggest objection seems to flow from Netflix's tendency to keep residuals and other profit participation types out of the hands of filmmakers, which would effectively reduce them to per-piece talent. If sufficient quantities of talent rebel, meanwhile, it could mean a big win for other streaming providers, and a huge potential loss for Netflix, which depends on fresh content to keep viewers.

Political Wrestlers?

Meanwhile, a recent event during All Elite Wrestling (AEW) saw Brody King removed from the schedule for a while. Some questioned whether or not it was related to anti-Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) chants before one of his matches. King has been clear in his opposition to ICE, but following the fans' chants of support, King did not appear on the following episode of AEW Dynamite, reports noted.

However, this was not deliberate, Warner noted. Warner clarified in a statement, saying, "Warner Bros. Discovery did not have any involvement in Brody King's upcoming AEW schedule. Any speculation to the contrary is categorically false. Brody is scheduled to appear during the next AEW event, which will air this Saturday on TNT and HBO Max."

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 171.86% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $28.86 per share implies 3.22% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
